stories.goal: add a Jira ticket to my local backlog via natural language
stories.benefit: I can curate a prioritized working set without leaving the CLI or manually copying ticket data

acceptance_criteria.given: the Atlassian MCP server is configured in Claude Code
acceptance_criteria.when: I say 'Add PROJ-1234 to the backlog'
acceptance_criteria.then: the framework pulls the ticket title, description, priority, and linked Confluence URLs from Jira and appends a new entry to BACKLOG.md with Jira metadata sub-bullets

acceptance_criteria.given: I add a Jira ticket to the backlog
acceptance_criteria.when: the entry is written to BACKLOG.md
acceptance_criteria.then: the entry includes the Jira ticket ID, title, priority, and any linked Confluence page URLs as metadata sub-bullets

acceptance_criteria.given: the Atlassian MCP server is NOT configured
acceptance_criteria.when: I try to add a Jira ticket
acceptance_criteria.then: I see a clear error message with MCP setup instructions (claude mcp add command) and the operation fails gracefully

acceptance_criteria.given: the Jira ticket ID does not exist
acceptance_criteria.when: I try to add it to the backlog
acceptance_criteria.then: I see a clear error message stating the ticket was not found

stories.goal: refresh my backlog to get latest ticket data from Jira
stories.benefit: my local backlog stays current with Jira without manual checking

acceptance_criteria.given: my BACKLOG.md contains Jira-backed items
acceptance_criteria.when: I say 'Refresh the backlog'
acceptance_criteria.then: the framework re-pulls title, description, priority, and status from Jira for all Jira-backed items and updates them in-place

acceptance_criteria.given: I refresh the backlog
acceptance_criteria.when: Jira data has changed (title, priority, status)
acceptance_criteria.then: BACKLOG.md entries are updated with the new Jira data while preserving local ordering and section placement

acceptance_criteria.given: I refresh the backlog
acceptance_criteria.when: some Jira tickets are unreachable (network error, permissions)
acceptance_criteria.then: the framework updates reachable tickets and reports which ones failed with clear error messages

stories.goal: reorder items in my backlog to reflect my priorities
stories.benefit: I control what I work on next without leaving the CLI

acceptance_criteria.given: my BACKLOG.md contains multiple items
acceptance_criteria.when: I say 'Move PROJ-1235 above PROJ-1234'
acceptance_criteria.then: the items are reordered in BACKLOG.md accordingly

acceptance_criteria.given: I reorder items locally
acceptance_criteria.when: the reorder completes
acceptance_criteria.then: the priority change is NOT synced back to Jira (local-only ordering)

stories.goal: start working on a backlog item and have the framework pull in Jira and Confluence context automatically
stories.benefit: the requirements agent starts from a position of knowledge instead of asking cold generic questions

acceptance_criteria.given: BACKLOG.md contains a Jira-backed item PROJ-1234
acceptance_criteria.when: I say 'Let's work on PROJ-1234'
acceptance_criteria.then: the framework finds the item in BACKLOG.md, determines workflow type (feature/fix), and initializes a workflow with the Jira ticket description as input

acceptance_criteria.given: Jira ticket PROJ-1234 links to a Confluence spec page
acceptance_criteria.when: the workflow reaches Phase 01 (requirements)
acceptance_criteria.then: the Confluence page content is pulled and injected as context for the requirements analyst agent

acceptance_criteria.given: I start a workflow from a Jira-backed backlog item
acceptance_criteria.when: the Confluence page is unavailable or not linked
acceptance_criteria.then: the workflow proceeds normally without Confluence context (graceful degradation)

acceptance_criteria.given: the Jira ticket type is 'Bug' or 'Defect'
acceptance_criteria.when: I start a workflow from this item
acceptance_criteria.then: the framework suggests a fix workflow; if 'Story' or 'Task', it suggests a feature workflow

stories.goal: have workflow completion automatically update Jira ticket status
stories.benefit: I don't have to manually update Jira after finishing work -- the framework keeps it in sync

acceptance_criteria.given: a workflow was started from a Jira-backed backlog item
acceptance_criteria.when: the workflow completes (branch merged to main)
acceptance_criteria.then: the Jira ticket status is transitioned to 'Done' (or configured completion status)

acceptance_criteria.given: a workflow completes for a Jira-backed item
acceptance_criteria.when: the status sync to Jira fails (permissions, workflow rules, network error)
acceptance_criteria.then: the workflow completion is NOT blocked; a warning is logged and the user is informed of the sync failure

acceptance_criteria.given: a workflow completes for a Jira-backed item
acceptance_criteria.when: status is synced back to Jira
acceptance_criteria.then: the BACKLOG.md entry is marked as [x] and moved to the Completed section

stories.goal: use the backlog without any external integrations
stories.benefit: I can manage my work locally even without Jira access

acceptance_criteria.given: the Atlassian MCP server is NOT configured
acceptance_criteria.when: I interact with BACKLOG.md (view, reorder, start workflow)
acceptance_criteria.then: all local backlog operations work normally for local-only items

acceptance_criteria.given: BACKLOG.md contains a mix of Jira-backed and local-only items
acceptance_criteria.when: the backlog picker presents options
acceptance_criteria.then: both types of items are shown and selectable

acceptance_criteria.given: I start a workflow from a local-only backlog item
acceptance_criteria.when: the workflow completes
acceptance_criteria.then: no Jira sync is attempted; the BACKLOG.md entry is marked as [x] normally

stories.goal: have CLAUDE.md guide the framework on backlog management conventions
stories.benefit: the framework handles backlog operations consistently via natural language without explicit commands

acceptance_criteria.given: iSDLC is installed in a project
acceptance_criteria.when: I check the generated CLAUDE.md
acceptance_criteria.then: it contains backlog management instructions: BACKLOG.md location, format conventions, Jira integration behavior, and Confluence context handling

acceptance_criteria.given: the CLAUDE.md contains backlog instructions
acceptance_criteria.when: I say something like 'add PROJ-1234 to the backlog' in conversation
acceptance_criteria.then: Claude detects the intent and performs the backlog operation per the CLAUDE.md instructions
